protocol.name: sayuki_project_v1
protocol.copyright: Copyright (c) 2026 Sayuki contributors. Licensed under MIT OR Apache-2.0.
protocol.description:
This protocol lets a trusted first-party client declare, before a toplevel maps, which Sayuki project (canvas) a surface belongs to and where it should sit in that canvas's free-floating coordinate space.
Sayuki organizes windows as a viewport over an unbounded canvas, one canvas per project. Without this protocol the compositor can only guess a surface's project affinity heuristically (by app_id or pid) after the fact; this protocol makes placement race-free and authoritative at map time.
Warning! The protocol described in this file is currently in the testing phase. Backward compatible changes may be added together with the corresponding interface version bump. Backward incompatible changes can only be done by creating a new major version of the extension.

interface.name: zsayuki_project_manager_v1
interface.description: The global object, advertised only to trusted first-party clients. It creates zsayuki_project_surface_v1 objects that tag a wl_surface with project affinity.

entry.name: already_constructed
entry.summary: the wl_surface already has a project-surface object

request.name: get_project_surface

description.summary: assign project affinity to a surface

request.description:
Create a project-surface object for the given wl_surface. The surface must have, or be about to be given, the xdg_toplevel role.
Requesting a second project-surface for a wl_surface that already has one is a protocol error (already_constructed).

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroy the manager object. Project-surface objects created by this manager are unaffected.

interface.name: zsayuki_project_surface_v1

description.summary: project affinity for a single surface

interface.description:
Declares the project a toplevel belongs to and its persistent canvas coordinates. All set_ requests must be sent before the surface's first commit (analogous to assigning an xdg role); sending them after the surface has mapped is a protocol error (already_mapped).
After placement the compositor reports the authoritative project name and canvas position the surface actually landed on.

entry.name: already_mapped
entry.summary: a set_ request was sent after the surface committed

request.name: set_project
request.description:
Set the name of the project (canvas) this surface should live on. If no project with this name is known the compositor places the surface on the current project instead and reports the fallback via the assigned event.
Must be sent before the first commit of the wl_surface.

request.name: set_canvas_position
request.description:
Set the surface's top-left position in the project canvas's free-floating coordinate space. The compositor may clamp the value to its canvas pan limits.
Must be sent before the first commit of the wl_surface.

request.name: set_rule_hint
request.description:
Provide a free-form window-rule hint (for example "floating", "pin", or "fullscreen") applied at map time. Unknown keys are ignored.
Must be sent before the first commit of the wl_surface.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroy the project-surface object. Affinity already applied at map time is retained; it is not re-evaluated on later commits.

event.name: assigned
event.description: Sent once the surface has been placed, carrying the name of the project canvas it actually landed on (which may differ from a requested but unknown project name).

event.name: canvas_position
event.description: Sent once the surface has been placed, carrying its final top-left position in the project canvas after any clamping.
